paper_title: Promoting learner autonomy: Lesson from using project work as a supplement in English skills courses

entities.id: Promoting learner autonomy: Lesson from using project work as a supplement in English skills courses
entities.description: The title and central study topic of the research paper.
The ability of learners to take charge of their own study, including decision-making and self-evaluation.
An educational approach claimed to develop autonomous learning skills and foreign language skills.
A process where students take initiative in their learning through planning and adjusting strategies.
Group tasks completed over 10-13 weeks to produce a specific product like a video or handbook.
A 26-item instrument used to measure components of learner autonomy like self-confidence and self-determination.
A qualitative data collection tool used to supplement quantitative findings from the questionnaire.
A statistical method used to analyze the significance of the increase in learner autonomy scores.
A statistical procedure used to identify key factors from the initial 45-item questionnaire.
The reliability coefficient calculated for the final version of the self-assessment questionnaire.
The p-value indicating that the overall increase in learner autonomy was statistically significant.
The measured difference in mean scores between the learner autonomy level before and after the program.
The primary research field focusing on teaching and training English language teachers.
The author of the research paper.
The academic organization in Vietnam where the research and author are based.

relations.source: Promoting learner autonomy: Lesson from using project work as a supplement in English skills courses
